macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Major Indexes Decline on February 27
items.summary: S&P 500 fell 0.46% to 6,877.36, Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.92% to 22,668.21, and Dow Jones lost 1.05% to 48,977.18 amid AI concerns and sector rotation into defensives.

items.title: S&P 500 Posts First Monthly Decline in Three Months
items.summary: S&P 500 slipped 0.87% in February, its largest drop since April 2025, while Nasdaq fell 2.32%; technology stocks led weakness due to AI valuation concerns.

items.title: Stocks Sink on AI Worries, Inflation Fears, and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Wall Street punished software and private equity firms vulnerable to AI disruption; oil prices rose 2.8% to $67.02 amid US-Iran tensions.

items.title: Treasuries Rally as Stocks Post February Losses
items.summary: Treasuries gained strongly on safe-haven demand while equities faced worst monthly performance since March; traders price in two Fed rate cuts this year despite hawkish FOMC minutes.

items.title: Netflix Surges After Dropping Warner Bros. Bid
items.summary: Netflix shares climbed 13.8% following its withdrawal from bidding on Warner Bros. Discovery’s studio and streaming business, boosting Paramount Skydance by 20.8%.

items.title: Fed Markets Price Two Rate Cuts Despite Hawkish Signals
items.summary: Bond markets fully price two Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026, up from December projections, as core PCE inflation hit 3%; FOMC minutes showed some hawkish discussions on hikes.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Crashes Below $64,000 Amid Market Liquidations and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin has plummeted over 6% in the past 24 hours, sliding toward $60,000, while Ethereum dropped nearly 10% to around $1,800. Over $88 million in BTC positions and $100 million in leveraged longs were liquidated as geopolitical tensions and disappointing economic data triggered panic selling.

items.title: Crypto Market Enters Fourth Winter as Bitcoin Down 48.5% from October Peak
items.summary: Bitcoin has lost 48.5% from its all-time high of $126,272 reached in October 2025, with the market now in its fourth crypto winter since 2009. Institutional support has weakened, with spot Bitcoin ETF assets under management falling by over $24 billion in the past month.

items.title: Stablecoins Emerge as Future of Crypto as Industry Seeks Real-Asset Backing
items.summary: Industry analysts predict stablecoins anchored to real assets will become the dominant form of cryptocurrency, with the stablecoin market cap expected to surpass Bitcoin before year-end. Regulatory focus is shifting toward market-structure legislation like the CLARITY Act.

items.title: Bitcoin Mining Stocks in Focus Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin-related stocks including IREN, Marathon Digital, TeraWulf, Cipher Mining, and Riot Platforms are among the most actively traded securities as investors monitor the crypto market downturn.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Signs Multi-Billion-Dollar Deal to Rent Google AI Chips
items.summary: Meta has entered a multi-year agreement to rent Google AI chips, underscoring the intensifying competition for AI compute resources amid surging demand for infrastructure.[4]

items.title: AI Scare Turns Real with Warnings of Mass Layoffs and White-Collar Recession
items.summary: Viral essays from Citrini Research and others predict a 'human intelligence displacement spiral' from rapid AI advances, leading to job losses for software engineers, financial advisors, and managers, though some tech CEOs dismiss it as hype.[2]

items.title: Debate Intensifies Over AI Job Displacement Panic
items.summary: Experts argue that AI panic over mass layoffs is overstated, citing historical tech adoption patterns and current uses of AI for automation in insurance claims processing with significant ROI, rather than widespread job replacement.[3]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pokemon Winds and Pokemon Waves Announced for Switch 2 in 2027
items.summary: The Pokemon Co. announced two new mainline Pokemon games coming to Nintendo Switch 2 in 2027, featuring open-world gameplay themed around windswept islands and ocean environments, marking the franchise's 30th anniversary.

items.title: Phil Spencer Retires from Microsoft After 38 Years; Asha Sharma Named New Gaming CEO
items.summary: Phil Spencer has retired from Microsoft effective February 23rd, with Asha Sharma, formerly president of Microsoft's CoreAI division, taking over as the new CEO of Microsoft Gaming. Sarah Bond, considered Spencer's likely successor, is also departing.

items.title: Minecraft Launches Safety Council with External Experts
items.summary: Minecraft announced the creation of a Safety Council composed of external experts to advise on trust and safety issues within the platform, representing an industry shift toward proactive safety practices.

items.title: Twitch Updates Community Moderation Approach
items.summary: Twitch announced changes to its enforcement system that will restrict access to specific features related to policy violations rather than suspending entire account access for less severe infractions.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Released for Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Resident Evil Requiem has launched on Nintendo Switch 2, marking the first day-one release of a mainline Resident Evil game on the platform and demonstrating strong Capcom support for the console.

items.title: Multiple Game Releases This Weekend: Raiden Fighters Remix Collection, FUR Squadron Phoenix, and More
items.summary: Several titles released this weekend including Raiden Fighters Remix Collection, Arcade Archives 2 Rave Racer, and FUR Squadron Phoenix (a Star Fox 64-style game), alongside a major Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 update introducing the Tech Marine class.

items.title: Mario Tennis Fever Released for Nintendo Switch 2
items.summary: Mario Tennis Fever launched on Nintendo Switch 2, featuring new gameplay mechanics including Fever Shots, defensive maneuvers, and Fever Rackets with special effects.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Block Announces 40% Workforce Reduction
items.summary: Jack Dorsey's Block is cutting 40% of its workforce as part of a strategic shift, with the company's stock rising nearly 14% following the announcement. This move reflects broader tech sector changes amid economic pressures.

items.title: Netflix Drops Bid for Warner Bros. Discovery, Clearing Path for Paramount-Skydance Deal
items.summary: Netflix withdrew its $87 billion bid for Warner Bros. Discovery, allowing Paramount Skydance to advance with a $110 billion acquisition of the entertainment giant including HBO and CNN.

items.title: NVIDIA Stock Declines After Earnings Amid OpenAI Investment Concerns
items.summary: NVIDIA shares fell 2% for a second day following earnings, with investors concerned over its $30 billion investment in OpenAI and growing competition in the sector.
